### p6570[0...7] BI: Precharging control signals signal sources

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8146, 9833, 9834 |
| **Object:**B_INFMV | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the control signals of the precharging.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Precharging on command from the sequence control  
[
1]:
Precharging circuit is switched on  
[
2]:
Precharging transformer temperature monitor OK  
[
3]:
Precharging off command external  
[
4]:
Precharging on command external  
[
5]:
Precharging on command from power semiconductor test  
[
6]:
Precharging ON command for DSM update  
[
7]:
Precharging fault, precharging converter

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6221

**Note:**
  
For index [2]:  
If there is no temperature monitor, then interconnect to a fixed value.  
For index [3]:  
When the binector input is interconnected, precharging is switched-on independent
of the magnitude of the precharging threshold.  
For index [5]:  
This index is only used for test operation of the power semiconductors (p6650 = 4).
The pre-charging setpoint refers to p6626.  
For index [7]:  
This index is exclusively implemented when employing precharging equipment using an
additional converter.

### r6571.0...8 CO/BO: Precharging control and status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9833, 9834 |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

5.2

**Description:**
  
Displays the control word and status word for precharging.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Activate precharging | OFF | ON | - |
| 01 | Precharging active | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Precharging complete | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Switch-on precharging for DSM update | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Switch-on precharging power semiconductor test | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Switch off precharging external | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Precharging switched on feedback | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Temperature monitor precharging transformer responded | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Precharging fault, precharging converter | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6570, p6623  
Refer to:
F05210, F30060, F30063

### p6620 DC link precharging test operation setpoint

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9834 |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [%] | 100.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 10.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the setpoint to precharge the DC link for test operation.  
The setpoint for the precharging refers to the setpoint of the DC link voltage (p3510).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3510, p6650

**Note:**
  
The pre-charging test mode is set using p6650 = 3.  
The two-level control (p6531 = 1) only functions for a setpoint above 10 %, as, at
this value, the lower threshold is greater than zero (p6622[1] > 0).

### p6621[0...n] DC link precharging setpoint

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 9833, 9834 |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 70.000 [%] | 150.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 85.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the setpoint for the DC link precharging.  
The setpoint for the precharging refers to the setpoint of the DC link voltage (p3510).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3510, p6626

**Note:**
  
In the semiconductor test mode (p6650 = 4), the setpoint for precharging refers to
p6626.

### r6622[0...1] CO: DC link precharging setpoint display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9833, 9834 |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [%] | - [%] | [ ] - [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the setpoint to precharge the DC link.  
The setpoint for the precharging refers to the setpoint of the DC link voltage (p3510/2).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Upper threshold  
[
1]:
Lower threshold

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6621

**Note:**
  
In the semiconductor test mode (p6650 = 4), the setpoint for precharging refers to
p6626.  
The value for the upper threshold is taken from p6621.  
The value for the lower threshold is calculated from hardware description data.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the DC link voltage actual value of the precharging.  
When precharging is active (r6571.1 = 1), the following applies:  
The actual value of the DC link voltage via signal source with index 0 is evaluated.  
When precharging is not active (r6571.1 = 0), the following applies:  
The actual value of the DC link voltage via signal source with index 1 is evaluated.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Actual value precharging active  
[
1]:
Actual value precharging inactive

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6571

**Note:**
  
A distinction is made between active and inactive precharging if index 1 was connected.
Otherwise, index number 0 is always valid.

### r6625[0...4] DC link voltages status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [V] | - [V] | [ ] - [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the status of the DC link voltages of the power unit.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
DC link voltage  
[
1]:
Permissible to reduce the DC link voltage  
[
2]:
DC link voltage from the supply voltage (p0210)  
[
3]:
DC link voltage reference  
[
4]:
DC link voltage from the actual supply voltage

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6639

**Note:**
  
For r6625[0]:  
Displays the DC link voltage corresponding with the voltage class of the power unit
(peak rectification).  
For r6625[1]:  
Displays the permissible DC link voltage, after taking into account the installation
altitude.  
For r6625[2]:  
Displays the DC link voltage resulting from the supply voltage (p0210).  
For r6625[3]:  
Display of the reference value of the DC link voltage is the same as r6625[2] for
a diode infeed or r6625[2] * step-up factor when the infeed is active.  
For r6625[4]:  
Displays the estimated (diode infeed) or measured (active infeed) from the DC link
voltage resulting from the actual supply voltage for peak rectification.  
Example:  
In order to explain the parameter indices and their interdependencies, the commissioning
of a fictitious power unit is described as an example. This involves a qualitative
example whose quantitative values are not generally valid.  
Assumption:  
Power unit with voltage class 3300 V, installation altitude 1000 m above sea level,
transformer tap at 3000 V, line supply voltage decreases, over the long term, by 5
%.  
Voltage class 3300 V:  
r6625[0] = sqrt(2) * 3300 V = 4667 V  
Before the installation altitude can be successfully entered into p6649, the line
supply voltage must be set to a lower value using p0210 (e.g. 2000 V).  
After p6649 = 1000 m is entered, the DC link voltage, resulting from the installation
altitude derating is taken into account.  
r6625[1] = sqrt(2) * 3300 V * 0.95 = 4433 V  
A transformer tap is now selected and therefore the supply voltage, whose peak rectification
value lies below r6625[1] (e.g. p0210 = 2800 V). This has the following result:  
r6625[2] = sqrt(2) * 2800 V = 3960 V  
If a drive with diode infeed is being used, the reference value of the DC link voltage
is also assigned this value; for a drive with active infeed, this is weighted with
the step-up factor.  
r6625[3] = sqrt(2) * 2800 V = 3960 V (diode infeed)  
r6625[3] = sqrt(2) * 2800 V * 1.08 = 4277 V (active infeed)  
Now, in operation, over the long term, the line supply voltage decreases by 5%:  
r6625[4] = sqrt(2) * 2800 V * 0.95 = 3762 V

### p6631 DC link voltage maximum value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 70.000 [%] | 125.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 110.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the trip threshold for a fault due to an overvoltage in the DC link.  
The threshold refers to the maximum permissible DC link voltage that is obtained taking
into consideration the installation altitude, depending on the device description
data (r6625[1]).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6625, p6627

### p6633 DC link voltage minimum value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8765 |
| **Object:**VECTORDM, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 50.000 [%] | 85.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 75.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the trip threshold for a fault due to an undervoltage in the DC link.  
The threshold refers to the maximum permissible DC link voltage that is obtained taking
into consideration the installation altitude, depending on the device description
data (r6625[1]).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6625, p6627

**Notice:**
  
For GH150, p6633 may be reduced to 50%. This means that operation can be maintained
even for undervoltage conditions if parameter p7532 (minimum DC link voltage on VECTORM2C)
is appropriately reduced.  
For all other products, only a value down to a minimum of 70% is permitted.

**Note:**
  
PSA: Power Stack Adapter

### p6642 Phase failure monitoring threshold value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORDM, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 100.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 5.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the threshold value for the phase failure monitoring function.  
For phase failure, pulsation occurs in the DC link with twice the line frequency.  
The percentage value is referred to the rated power (hardware description data).  
Fault F30029 is output when the set threshold value is exceeded.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6641  
Refer to:
F30029

**Note:**
  
Phase failure monitoring is deactivated for p6642 = 0.00 %.

### p6650 Sequence control test mode activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**B_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 42 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate/deactivate test mode for sequence control.  
For test mode [80]: the CU and DSM should be switched off and on again after the update.

**Value:**
  
0:
Test operation inactive  
3:
Test operation precharging  
6:
Test operation redundant fan  
10:
Test operation cooling  
12:
Test operation additional auxiliaries  
15:
Test operation line supply monitoring  
20:
Test operation line side switch 1  
40:
Test operation precharging and circuit breaker  
42:
Test mode premagnetization

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0840, p6651  
Refer to:
A07318

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
the test mode.

**Caution:**
  
Bit r0899.3 (fault present) can be set with delay in the status word due to the message
generation.  
For protection reasons, the "Precharging" test mode is limited from a time perspective,
as operation without cooling is possible.

**Notice:**
  
For p6650 = 20 and GH150 parallel connection:  
Relay K14 or K112 must be jumpered in order to obtain an enable signal for the switch.

**Note:**
  
The test is started via binector input p6651 = 0/1 signal.  
For p6650 = 15:  
Activates the line voltage monitoring (function diagram 8761, 8762) when the converter
is ready to be switched on (p3402 = 200).

### r6660.0...5 CO/BO: Switch 0 control signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays (binector outputs) the control signals for switch 0.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | "Close" command | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | "Open" command | Active | Inactive | - |
| 02 | Command "open undervoltage coil" | Active | Inactive | - |
| 03 | Command "open higher-level switch" | Inactive | Active | - |
| 04 | Software feedback signal closed | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Software feedback signal open | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 03:  
This signal is set if, as a result of the command "open switch", no feedback signal
was detected and the crowbar thyristor was fired. It can be used to switch off the
plant/system by means of a higher-level switch.

### p6670[0...5] BI: Switch simulation mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source of the simulation mode for the switch.  
When the simulation mode is activated, the feedback signals for the switch are set
the same as the software commands. All monitoring functions are deactivated.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
the simulation mode.

**Note:**
  
BI: p6670[x] = 1 signal: Simulation mode activated  
BI: p6670[x] = 0 signal: Simulation mode deactivated

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

4.7

### p6675[0...5] Switch open monitoring time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.030 [s] | 20.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.160 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the monitoring time of the feedback signals when opening the switch.  
The feedback signal within the time in p6675 must indicate that the switch has reliably
opened (i.e. bounce can no longer be detected). The monitoring time in p6675 must
be set greater than the time in p6676 as it additionally takes into account the bounce
time of the feedback signal.  
The following applies: p6675 > p6676

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6674, p6676  
Refer to:
F07311

**Danger:**
  
If this monitoring function responds, then it must be assumed that the switch had
not opened. In this case, the drive system is not disconnected from the line supply.

### p6676[0...5] Switch max. opening time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.030 [s] | 20.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.100 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum permissible opening time of the switch.  
The feedback signal must indicate for the first time that the switch has opened within
the time in p6676. The feedback signal must be stable within the time in p6675 (i.e.
bounce can no longer be detected).  
The following applies: p6675 > p6676

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6675  
Refer to:
F07306

**Danger:**
  
The switch used must open within this time in order to protect the drive system.  
Switch with a longer opening time than specified in this parameter cannot be used.

### p6682[0...5] Switch inhibit time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [s] | 10.00 [s] | [ 0 ] 2.00 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the inhibit time for the switch.  
After opening, this inhibit time is started. The switch cannot be closed while this
time is running and the missing ready signal is suppressed.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| 01 | Feedback signal switch closed | No | Yes | - |

| 03 | Feedback signal switch auxiliary voltage 1 OK | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Feedback signal switch auxiliary voltage 2 OK | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Feedback signal no external trip | No | Yes | - |

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| 04 | Auxiliary voltage 1 missing | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Auxiliary voltage 2 missing | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Consistency error | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Crowbar thyristor and feedback signal missing | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | External trip | No | Yes | - |

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

**Valid as of version:**

**Description:**
  
Sets the lower alarm threshold to monitor the number of times that the switch is operated.  
The lower threshold defines the output for the first alarm.

[
0]:

[
1]:
Switch 1

2]:
Switch 2  
[

Switch 3  
[
4]:

**Notice:**
  
After this alarm is output for the first time, the parameters must be set corresponding
to the switching being used.

**Note:**
  
For p6710 = 0, the monitoring for the lower alarm threshold is switched out.

### p6711[0...5] Switch switching alarm threshold upper

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the upper alarm threshold to monitor the number of times that the switch is operated.  
The upper threshold defines the output for the second alarm.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A07323

**Caution:**
  
After this alarm is output for the first time, the parameters must be set corresponding
to the switching being used.  
When this alarm threshold is exceeded, the stress on the switch must be checked and
if required, the switch should be replaced.

**Note:**
  
For p6711 = 0, the monitoring for the upper alarm threshold is switched out.

### r6713[0...5] Switch switching operations, total

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays the total number of switching operations of the switch.  
The total switching operations are determined as follows:  
r6713 = r6714 + r6715 * p6712  
r6713: Switching operations, total  
r6714: Switching operations in a no-current condition  
r6715: Switching operations under load  
p6712: Weighting factor for switching operations under load

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6710, p6711, p6712, p6714, p6715  
Refer to:
A07322, A07323

### p6714[0...5] Switch switching operations in a no-current condition

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 100000 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays and sets the number of switching operations of the switch under no-current
conditions.  
When setting the parameter, the value is accepted without confirmation.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6713, p6715  
Refer to:
A07322, A07323

**Note:**
  
The total number of switching operations of the switch under no-current conditions
is saved in a non-volatile fashion on all of the Power Stack Adapters (PSA) being
used.  
Inconsistency is not monitored and there is no automatic alignment of the switching
operations for several Power Stack Adapters. The values of the first Power Stack Adapter
are read and evaluated.

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [Pa] | 1000.00 [Pa] | [ 0 ] 600.00 [Pa] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the differential pressure for the alarm threshold for the main fan.  
This value is an absolute value with units of Pascal (Pa). The other parameter values
refer to this.  
Alarm A49111 is output when this differential pressure is fallen below.  
When the differential pressure is fallen below, the redundant main fan is simultaneously
switched on (if one exists) and Alarm A49118 is output.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Differential pressure actual value 1  
[
1]:
Differential pressure actual value 2 (optional)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6791  
Refer to:
A49111

2.6

0]:

### p6792[0...3] Main fan minimum differential pressure change when switching

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the minimum differential pressure change for the alarm threshold when switching
on/switching off the fans.  
If at least this differential pressure is not established within the monitoring time
when switching on/switching off, then an appropriate alarm is output.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Main fan differential pressure actual value 1  
[
1]:
Redundant main fan diff. pressure actual value 1 (optional)  
[
2]:
Main fan differential pressure actual value 2 (optional)  
[
3]:
Redundant main fan diff. pressure actual value 2 (optional)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6790, p6794  
Refer to:
A49114, A49115

### p6793[0...1] Main fan differential press. change when filter mats are clogged

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Fan), VECTOR3P (Fan), VECTORSL (Fan), A_INFMV (Fan), B_INFMV (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.10 [%] | 100.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 2.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the minimum differential pressure change when the filter mats are clogged.  
If the differential pressure, during operation, enters a band above the alarm threshold
(p6790 + p6793) without such a differential pressure change occurring, then an appropriate
alarm is output as the filter mats are clogged.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Differential pressure actual value 1  
[
1]:
Differential pressure actual value 2 (optional)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6790, p6796  
Refer to:
A49117

### p6796[0...1] Main fan, differential pressure tolerance

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9805 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Fan), VECTOR3P (Fan), VECTORSL (Fan), A_INFMV (Fan), B_INFMV (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 20.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 5.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the tolerance for the differential pressure.  
This tolerance value is used in the following cases:  
- hysteresis for the prewarning threshold for clogged filter mats (p6790 + 2 x p6796),
A49117.  
- hysteresis for the alarm threshold of the main fan and the switch-on threshold of
the redundant main fan (p6790 + p6796), A49111, A49118.  
- plausibility monitoring differential pressure actual value (zero - p6796, hysteresis
1 Pa), F49112.  
- plausibility monitoring differential pressure actual value (maximum pressure actual
value 1000 Pa - p6796, hysteresis p6796 x p6790), A49115.  
- hysteresis value for alarm A49111, A49118.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Differential pressure actual value 1  
[
1]:
Differential pressure actual value 2 (optional)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6790  
Refer to:
A49111, F49112, A49115, A49117, A49118

## SINAMICS Parameter BASIC INFEED CONTROLMV 06814 - 08893

SINAMICS Parameter BASIC INFEED CONTROLMV 06814 - 08893

### p6814 Braking chopper DC link voltage threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7622 |
| **Object:**VECTORDM, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 30.00 [%] | 135.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 120.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the overvoltage threshold in the DC link.  
The value is referred to r6625[1].  
If the actual value of the DC link voltage r0070[0] exceeds this threshold, then a
negative current setpoint is generated for the braking chopper and is made available
at connector output r6818 for interconnection; for example, to switch on the braking
chopper.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6817, r6818

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 20 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the current setpoint scaling for the braking chopper.  
This value scales the measured overvoltage for a current setpoint for the braking
chopper.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6814, r6818

| 00 | Internal activation | Not set | Set | - |
| 01 | External activation | Not set | Set | - |
| 02 | Internal trigger | Not set | Set | - |
| 03 | External trigger 1.1 | Not set | Set | - |
| 04 | External trigger 1.2 | Not set | Set | - |

| 08 | External trigger 2.2 | Not set | Set | - |
| 09 | External trigger 2.3 | Not set | Set | - |

| 11 | Data buffering running | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Post trigger time running | No | Yes | - |

### p6993[0...5] Recorder trigger 2 bit mask

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | FFFF FFFF hex | [ 0 ] 0001 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the bit mask for trigger signal 2 (p6994) of the recorder.  
External trigger 2.1 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[0] and the bit mask in p6993[0].  
External trigger 2.2 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[1] and the bit mask in p6993[1].  
External trigger 2.3 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[2] and the bit mask in p6993[2].  
External trigger 2.4 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[3] and the bit mask in p6993[3].  
External trigger 2.5 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[4] and the bit mask in p6993[4].  
External trigger 2.6 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[5] and the bit mask in p6993[5].

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External trigger 2.1  
[
1]:
External trigger 2.2  
[
2]:
External trigger 2.3  
[
3]:
External trigger 2.4  
[
4]:
External trigger 2.5  
[
5]:
External trigger 2.6

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6994

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for trigger 2 of the recorder.  
External trigger 2.1 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in

External trigger 2.2 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[1] and the bit mask in p6993[1].  
External trigger 2.3 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[2] and the bit mask in p6993[2].  
External trigger 2.4 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[3] and the bit mask in p6993[3].  
External trigger 2.5 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[4] and the bit mask in p6993[4].  
External trigger 2.6 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[5] and the bit mask in p6993[5].

**Index:**
  
[

External trigger 2.1  
[
1]:
External trigger 2.2

2]:
External trigger 2.3  
[
3]:
External trigger 2.4

4]:

10:
Active

Prepare data save operation

Start data save  
50:
End data save  
60:
Configuration

### p6998[0...4] BI: Recorder trigger 1 signal sources

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |

| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources to activate and trigger the recorder.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External activation  
[
1]:
External trigger 1.1  
[
2]:
External trigger 1.2  
[
3]:
External trigger 1.3  
[
4]:
External trigger 1.4

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.  
For index [0]:  
The external recorder activation extends the internal activation. If the recorder
has already been activated internally, then it can be externally triggered via p6998[1..4]
or p6994[0..2], without requiring that p6998[0] = 1.

### p6999[0...8] Recorder parameterization

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144, 8145 |
| **Object:**B_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2000 | [ 0 ] 1   [ 1 ] 2000   [ 2 ] 1900   [ 3 ] 0   [ 4 ] 0   [ 5 ] 100   [ 6 ] 0   [ 7 ] 1   [ 8 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Setting to parameterize the recorder.  
The recorder supplies up to 256 internal variables (depending on the parameterization).
The maximum recording time is 2000 ms. The variables are acquired in an integer multiple
of the current controller sampling time – and a pretrigger can be set. The values
can then be acyclically written to the memory card.  
The development and system test departments have the software necessary to decode
the content.  
Normally, this recorder is always enabled. However, if problems occur for topologies
with a low remaining CPU time, then it can be deactivated using this parameter (this
saves approx. 5 ... 20 µs CPU time).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Enable  
[
1]:
Recording time  
[
2]:
Pretrigger time internal trigger  
[
3]:
Output message  
[
4]:
Recording factor  
[
5]:
Pretrigger time external trigger  
[
6]:
Hardware trace in synchronism  
[
7]:
Hardware trigger active  
[
8]:
Recording sampling time factor

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49998

**Note:**
  
For index [0]:  
Enables or disables the function.  
p6999[0] = 0  
Inhibits the function.  
p6999[0] = 1  
Enables the function. A POWER ON is required after the enable.  
p6999[0] = 20  
Sets the factory setting for p6999, p6996, p6993, p6994, p6998.  
For index [1]:  
Sets the recording time, [ms]. The value is discretized with the current controller
sampling time.  
For index [2]:  
Sets the pretrigger time [ms] of the internal trigger. This time is included in the
recording time and cannot be longer than the recording time p6999[1]. The value is
discretized with the current controller sampling time.  
For index [3]:  
Enables or inhibits the output of message A49998 when a trigger event is initiated.  
For index [4]:  
p6999[4] = n, n = 0 ... 4  
Recording with the factor, which extends the record time p6999[1] and the pre-trigger
time p6999[2] 2^n times, and reduces the number of signals 2^n times.  
Example:  
p6999[1] = 2000, p6999[2] = 1000, p6999[4] = 4  
--> recording time: 2000 ms * 2^4 = 32 s, pre-trigger time:1000 ms * 2^4 = 16 s, number
of signals 128/16 = 8  
When setting p6999[4] to 0, the results are as follows:  
--> recording time: 2000 ms, pre-trigger time: 1000 ms, number of signals: 128  
The maximum number of signals depends on the drive object.  
For index [5]:  
Sets the pretrigger time [ms] of the external trigger. This time is included in the
recording time and cannot be longer than the recording time p6999[1]. The value is
discretized with the current controller sampling time.  
For index [6]:  
DFEMV has no Power Stack Adapter (PSA). Waiting for the upload to be completed means
that the recorder remains in state 15 "Data transfer locked" As a consequence, the
index is set to 0 as default, and should not be written to.  
For index [7]:  
Deactivates/activates the hardware trigger. When p6999[7] = 0, the hardware trigger
is deactivated, and a hardware fault means that the recorder is no longer triggered.  
For index [8]:  
Sets the factor for the recording sampling time.  
Example:  
p6999[8] = 2 --> sampling time to record/trace signals is 2 x current controller sampling
time

### r7108[0...7] Fault buffer fault code system 2

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

**Description:**
  
Displays the relative system runtime in days when the fault/alarm occurred.  
Ring buffer for faults and alarms that have occurred for power units connected in
parallel.

The time display depends on the selected mode (p3100).

**Note:**
  
The last fault case that occurred is documented in index 0.  
The parameter is reset to 0 at POWER ON.

### r7760 Write protection/know-how protection status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, BMM2C, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the status for the write protection and know-how protection.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Write protection active | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Know-how protection active | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Know-how protection temporarily withdrawn | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Know-how protection cannot be deactivated | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Extended copy protection is active | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Basic copy protection is active | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Trace and measuring functions for diagnostic purposes active | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Reserved, Siemens-internal | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p7761, p7765

**Note:**
  
KHP: Know-How Protection  
For bit 00:  
Write protection can be activated/deactivated via p7761 on the Control Unit.  
For bit 01:  
The know-how protection can be activated by entering a password (p7766 ... p7768).  
For bit 02:  
If it has already been activated, know-how protection can be temporarily deactivated
by entering the valid password in p7766. In this case, bit 1 = 0 and bit 2 = 1 offset.  
For bit 03:  
Know-how protection cannot be deactivated, as p7766 is not entered in the OEM exception
list (only the factory setting is possible). This bit is only set if know-how protection
is active (bit 1 = 1) and p7766 has not been entered in the OEM exception list.  
For bit 04:  
When know-how protection has been activated, the contents of the memory card (parameter
and DCC data) can be additionally protected against being used with other memory cards/Control
Units. This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and in p7765.0 is set
= 1.  
For bit 05:  
When know-how protection has been activated, the contents of the memory card (parameter
and DCC data) can be additionally protected against being used with other memory cards.
This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and p7765.1 is set = 1 and p7765.0
is set = 0.  
For bit 06:  
When know-how protection is activated, the drive data can be traced using the device
trace function. This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and p7765.2
is set = 1.  
For bit 12:  
Together with p7755, the bit is used to monitor write protection.  
Bit = 1, if p7755 is not equal to 0 and write protection is active (r7760.0 = 1).  
Bit = 0, if write protection was deactivated. p7755 is set to 0, and when write protection
is activated again, bit 12 remains at 0.

to.

### p7770 NVRAM action

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the action to be executed for NVRAM data.  
At the end of the action the value is automatically set to 0.

**Value:**
  
0:
Inactive  
1:
Load NVRAM data to parameters  
2:
Load parameters to NVRAM  
3:
Reset

**Notice:**
  
After action p7770 = 1 no more pulses may be enabled.  
After action p7770 = 2, it is essential that parameters are backed up (p0977 = 1)
and that a warm restart is then performed (p0009 = 30, p0976 = 2, 3). This will apply
the values written.

**Note:**
  
For value = 1:  
This action loads the NVRAM data to the parameters.  
For value = 2:  
This action loads the parameters to the NVRAM.  
If value = 3:  
This action sets parameters p7771 ... p7774 to the factory setting.  
It is recommended to avoid placing unnecessary load on the subsequent upload/download
operation.

### r7871[0...15] Configuration changes drive object

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the configuration changes on the drive object.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Sum of the following indices  
[
1]:
p0010, p0107, p0108  
[
2]:
Drive object name (p0199)  
[
3]:
Structure-relevant parameters (e.g. p0180)  
[
4]:
BICO interconnections  
[
5]:
Activate/deactivate drive object  
[
6]:
Data backup required  
[
7]:
Activate/deactivate component  
[
8]:
Reference or changeover parameters (e.g. p2000)  
[
9]:
Parameter count through Drive Control Chart (DCC)  
[
10]:
p0107, p0108  
[
11]:
Reserved  
[
12]:
Write protection and know-how protection status  
[
13]:
Reserved  
[
14]:
Reserved  
[
15]:
Reserved

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7868, r7870

**Note:**
  
For index [0]:  
When changing one of the following indices, then the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [1]:  
Drive object commissioning: When changing p0010, p0107, p0108, p0171, p0172 or p0173,
the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [2]:  
Drive object name. When changing p0199, the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [3]:  
Drive object structure. When changing a parameter that is relevant for the structure
(e.g. number of data sets), the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [4]:  
Drive object BICO interconnections. When changing r3977, the value in this index is
incremented.  
For index [5]:  
Drive object activity: When changing p0105, the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [6]:  
Drive object, data save.  
0: There are no parameter changes to save.  
1: There are parameter changes to save.  
For index [7]:  
Drive object component activity: When changing either p0125 or p0145, the value in
this index is incremented.  
For index [8]:  
Drive object changeover of units. When changing reference or changeover parameters
(e.g. p2000, p0304), the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [9]:  
Drive object parameter count. When changing the number of parameters by loading Drive
Control Chart (DCC), the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [10]:  
Drive object configuration. When changing p0107, p0108, p0171, p0172 or p0173, the
value in this index is incremented.

### p8612[0...1] CAN drive object server SDO

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN (CAN), CU_MV_DP (CAN), VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0581 hex | 8000 067F hex | [ 0 ] 8000 0000 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the identifier (client/server and server/client) of the additional SDO server.  
Using these SDO servers, it is possible to access manufacturer-specific CANopen objects
of the supported drive objects.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
COB-ID drive object from the client to the server  
[
1]:
COB-ID drive object from the server to the client

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r8610

**Note:**
  
SDO: Service Data Object  
Regarding the drive object Control Unit:  
- Corresponds to the CANopen object 1201 hex  
Regarding the drive object with closed-loop control functions:  
- Corresponds to the CANopen object 1202 hex + 1 * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7)

### p8641 CAN Abort Connection Option Code

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 3 |

4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the fault response for a CAN communication error with output of fault F08700.  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 6007 hex.

**Value:**
  
0:
No response  
1:
OFF1  
2:
OFF2  
3:
OFF3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p2100, p2101  
Refer to:
F08700

**Note:**
  
When the factory setting is changed, the fault response of fault F08700 is written
to p2100 and p2101. In this particular case it is not possible to change the message
type using p2118 and p2119.

### p8700[0...1] CAN Receive PDO 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

1).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:

[
1]:
PDO transmission type

A valid COB-ID can only be set for the available (existing) channel.

Corresponds to the CANopen object 1400 hex + 40 hex * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7).  
Transmission types 0, 1, FE and FF can be set.  
PDO: Process Data Object

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the communication parameters for CANopen Receive Process Data Object 3 (RPDO
3).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PDO COB-ID  
[
1]:
PDO transmission type

**Dependency:**
  
A valid COB-ID can only be set for the available (existing) channel.

**Note:**
  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 1402 hex + 40 hex * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7).  
Transmission types 0, 1, FE and FF can be set.  
PDO: Process Data Object

### p8720[0...4] CAN Transmit PDO 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 3) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9208, 9210 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | C000 06DF hex | [ 0 ] C000 06DF hex   [ 1 ] 00FE hex   [ 2 ] 0000 hex   [ 3 ] 0000 hex   [ 4 ] 0000 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the communication parameters for CANopen Transmit Process Data Object 1 (TPDO
1).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PDO COB-ID  
[
1]:
PDO transmission type  
[
2]:
Inhibit time (in 100 µs)  
[
3]:
Reserved  
[
4]:
Event timer (in ms)

**Dependency:**
  
A valid COB-ID can only be set for the available (existing) channel.

**Notice:**
  
For inhibit time and event timer, the following apply:  
A value that is not a multiple integer of the CANopen sampling time is rounded-off.

**Note:**
  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 1800 hex + 40 hex * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7).  
Transmission types 0, 1 ... F0, FE and FF can be set.  
p8848: CANopen sampling time  
PDO: Process Data Object

4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the communication parameters for CANopen Transmit Process Data Object 3 (TPDO
3).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PDO COB-ID  
[
1]:
PDO transmission type  
[
2]:
Inhibit time (in 100 µs)  
[
3]:
Reserved  
[
4]:
Event timer (in ms)

**Dependency:**
  
A valid COB-ID can only be set for the available (existing) channel.

**Notice:**
  
For inhibit time and event timer, the following apply:  
A value that is not a multiple integer of the CANopen sampling time is rounded-off.

**Note:**
  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 1802 hex + 40 hex * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7).  
Transmission types 0, 1 ... F0, FE and FF can be set.  
p8848: CANopen sampling time  
PDO: Process Data Object

### r8745[0...15] CO: CAN free PZD receive objects 16 bit

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**4000H | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output of the free PZD receive objects (16 bit) using SDO transfer.  
An index does not contain a sensible value if the corresponding object is mapped in
one of the 8 possible RPDO (p8710 ... p8717). In this case, the interconnection and
the SDO transfer must be realized via PZD interface 2 (r8850[x]).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PZD object 0  
[
1]:
PZD object 1  
[
2]:
PZD object 2  
[
3]:
PZD object 3  
[
4]:
PZD object 4  
[
5]:
PZD object 5  
[
6]:
PZD object 6  
[
7]:
PZD object 7  
[
8]:
PZD object 8  
[
9]:
PZD object 9  
[
10]:

### p8837 IF2 STW1.10 = 0 mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, ENC | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 2 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the processing mode for PROFIdrive STW1.10 "master control by PLC".  
Generally, control world 1 is received with the first receive word (PZD1) (this is
in conformance to the PROFIdrive profile). The behavior of STW1.10 = 0 corresponds
to that of the PROFIdrive profile. For other applications that deviate from this,
the behavior can be adapted using this particular parameter.

**Recommend.:**
  
Do not change the setting p2037 = 0.

**Value:**
  
0:
Freeze setpoints and continue to process sign-of-life  
1:
Freeze setpoints and sign-of-life  
2:
Do not freeze setpoints

**Note:**
  
If the STW1 is not transferred according to the PROFIdrive with PZD1 (with bit 10
"master control by PLC"), then p2037 should be set to 2.

### p8844 IF2 fault delay

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2410 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, ENC | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [s] | 100 [s] | [ 0 ] 0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the delay time to initiate fault F01910 after a setpoint failure.  
The time until the fault is initiated can be used by the application. This means that
it is possible to respond to the failure while the drive is still operational (e.g.
emergency retraction).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r2043  
Refer to:
F01910

**Notice:**
  
Where there is a multiple interconnection of a connector output, all the connector
inputs must either have Integer or FloatingPoint data types.  
A BICO interconnection for a single PZD can only take place either on r8850 or r8860.

**Note:**
  
IF2: Interface 2  
PZD1 to PZD4 are displayed bit-serially in r8890 to r8893.

Where there is a multiple interconnection of a connector output, all the connector
inputs must either have Integer or FloatingPoint data types.  
A BICO interconnection for a single PZD can only take place either on r8850 or r8860.  
A maximum of 4 indices of the "trace" function can be used.

### p8870[0...31] SINAMICS Link PZD receive word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2198, 2199 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (PN CBE20), VECTORGL (PN CBE20), VECTOR3P (PN CBE20), VECTORSL (PN CBE20), VECTORM2C (PN CBE20), VECTORDM (PN CBE20), A_INFMV (PN CBE20), B_INFMV (PN CBE20), A_INFM2C (PN CBE20), BMM2C (PN CBE20) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Assignment of a PZD to a telegram word from a SINAMICS Link receive telegram.  
For p8839[0] = 2 (COMM BOARD via interface 1), the following applies:  
- PZD p2050[index] is assigned by means of p8870[index], p8872[index].  
For p8839[1] = 2 (COMM BOARD via interface 2), the following applies:  
- using p8870[index], p8872[index], the PZD is assigned r8850[Index].

A pair of values p8870[index], p8872[index] may only be used once in single a device.  
A change only becomes effective after POWER ON, reset, project download or p8842 =
1.

### p8888[0...4] IF2 invert binector-connector converter status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2489 |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, BMM2C, ENC | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to invert the individual binector inputs of the binector connector converter.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Status word 1  
[
1]:
Status word 2  
[
2]:
Free status word 3  
[
3]:
Free status word 4  
[
4]:
Free status word 5

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Bit 0 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 01 | Bit 1 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 02 | Bit 2 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 03 | Bit 3 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 04 | Bit 4 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 05 | Bit 5 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 06 | Bit 6 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 07 | Bit 7 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 08 | Bit 8 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 09 | Bit 9 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 10 | Bit 10 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 11 | Bit 11 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 12 | Bit 12 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 13 | Bit 13 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 14 | Bit 14 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 15 | Bit 15 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p8880, p8881, p8882, p8883, p8884, r8889

3]:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Bit 0 | OFF | ON | - |
| 01 | Bit 1 | OFF | ON | - |
| 02 | Bit 2 | OFF | ON | - |
| 03 | Bit 3 | OFF | ON | - |
| 04 | Bit 4 | OFF | ON | - |
| 05 | Bit 5 | OFF | ON | - |
| 06 | Bit 6 | OFF | ON | - |
| 07 | Bit 7 | OFF | ON | - |
| 08 | Bit 8 | OFF | ON | - |
| 09 | Bit 9 | OFF | ON | - |
| 10 | Bit 10 | OFF | ON | - |
| 11 | Bit 11 | OFF | ON | - |
| 12 | Bit 12 | OFF | ON | - |
| 13 | Bit 13 | OFF | ON | - |
| 14 | Bit 14 | OFF | ON | - |
| 15 | Bit 15 | OFF | ON | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p8851, p8880, p8881, p8882, p8883, p8884, p8888

**Note:**
  
r8889 together with p8880 to p8884 forms five binector-connector converters.

### p8898[0...1] IF2 invert connector-binector converter binector output

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2485, 2491 |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, BMM2C, ENC | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to invert the individual binector outputs of the connector-binector converter.  
Using p8898[0], the signals of CI: p8899[0] are influenced.  
Using p8898[1], the signals of CI: p8899[1] are influenced.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Bit 0 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 01 | Bit 1 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 02 | Bit 2 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 03 | Bit 3 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 04 | Bit 4 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 05 | Bit 5 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 06 | Bit 6 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 07 | Bit 7 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 08 | Bit 8 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 09 | Bit 9 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 10 | Bit 10 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 11 | Bit 11 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 12 | Bit 12 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 13 | Bit 13 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 14 | Bit 14 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 15 | Bit 15 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r8894, r8895, p8899

### p8899[0...1] CI: IF2 connector-binector converter signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2485, 2491 |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, BMM2C, ENC | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the connector-binector converter.  
A PZD receive word can be selected as signal source. The signals are available to
be serially passed-on (interconnection).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r8850, r8894, r8895, p8898

**Note:**
  
From the signal source set via the connector input, the corresponding lower 16 bits
are converted.  
p8899[0...1] together with r8894.0...15 and r8895.0...15 forms two connector-binector
converters:  
Connector input p8899[0] to binector output in r8894.0...15  
Connector input p8899[1] to binector output in r8895.0...15

### r8960[0...3] PN subslot controller assignment

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, BMM2C, ENC | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 8 | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Displays the controller assignment of a PROFINET subslot on the actual drive object.  
The display is only relevant for Shared Device.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Subslot 2 PROFIsafe  
[
1]:
Subslot 3 PZD telegram  
[
2]:
Subslot 4 PZD supplementary data  
[
3]:
Subslot 5 PZD supplementary data

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r8961, r8962

**Note:**
  
Example:  
If the parameter contains the value 2 in index [1], then this means that subslot 3
is assigned to controller 2.

### r9406[0...19] PS file parameter number parameter not transferred

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the parameters that were not able to be transferred when reading the parameter
back-up files (PS files) from the non-volatile memory (e.g. memory card).  
r9406[0] = 0  
--> All of the parameter values were able to be transferred error-free.  
r9406[0...x] > 0  
--> indicates the parameter number in the following cases:  
- parameter, whose value was not able to be completely accepted.  
- indexed parameter, where at least 1 index was not able to be accepted. The first
index that is not transferred is displayed in r9407.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r9407, r9408

**Note:**
  
All indices from r9406 to r9408 designate the same parameter.  
r9406[x] parameter number, parameter not accepted  
r9407[x] parameter index, parameter not accepted  
r9408[x] fault code, parameter not accepted

### r9407[0...19] PS file parameter index parameter not transferred

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the first index of the parameters that could not be transferred when the
parameter backup files (PS files) were read from the non-volatile memory (e.g. memory
card).  
If, from an indexed parameter, at least one index was not able to be transferred,
then the parameter number is displayed in r9406[n] and the first index that was not
transferred is displayed in r9407[n].  
r9406[0] = 0  
--> All of the parameter values were able to be transferred error-free.  
r9406[n] > 0  
--> Displays r9407[n] the first index of the parameter number r9406[n] that was not
transferred.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r9406, r9408

**Note:**
  
All indices from r9406 to r9408 designate the same parameter.  
r9406[x] parameter number, parameter not accepted  
r9407[x] parameter index, parameter not accepted  
r9408[x] fault code, parameter not accepted

### r9409 Number of parameters to be saved

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the number of modified parameters and those that have still not be saved
for this drive object.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0971, p0977

**Notice:**
  
Inherent to the system, the list of the parameters to be backed up is empty after
the following actions:  
- Download  
- Warm restart  
- Factory setting  
In these cases, a new parameter backup must be initiated, which is then the starting
point for the list of modified parameters.

**Note:**
  
The modified parameters that still need to be saved are internally listed in r9410
... r9419.

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the signal receiver list (Binector Input/Connector Input, BI/CI) for the
first interconnections between this drive and other drives/drive objects.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r9490, r9492, p9493

**Notice:**
  
A drive cannot be deleted if this list is not empty!  
Otherwise, another drive would continue to attempt to read a signal from a drive that
no longer existed.

**Note:**
  
All indices of r9491 to p9493 designate the same interconnection.  
r9491[x] contains the signal receiver and r9492[x] the matching signal source; p9493[x]
can be set to modify the interconnection.

**Notice:**
  
A drive cannot be deleted if this list is not empty!  
Otherwise, another drive would continue to attempt to read a signal from a drive that

**Note:**
  
All indices of r9491 to p9493 designate the same interconnection.  
r9491[x] contains the signal receiver and r9492[x] the matching signal source; p9493[x]

**Valid as of version:**
  
2.6

**Description:**
  
Setting to reset the BICO interconnections to other drives.  
Each interconnection can be individually reset.

**Value:**
  
0:
Set connection to 0  
1:
Set connection to 1 (100 %)  
2:
Set connection to factory setting  
15:
Finished

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r9490, r9491, r9492

**Note:**
  
All indices of r9491 to p9493 designate the same interconnection.  
r9491[x] contains the signal receiver and r9492[x] the matching signal source; p9493[x]
can be set to modify the interconnection.

|  |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for rescaling from specific to power-referred.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0514, p0521, r17521

**Note:**
  
This parameter is entered as standard in p0521[0...19].  
As a consequence, the reference value entered in p0514[6] is applicable for the surge
power.  
Background:  
To exchange process quantities between drive objects with various reference variables,
it is necessary to scale to a common reference variable. Generally, the same common
reference value (e.g. p0514[6]) is set on the send and receive sides.

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

[
1]:

[
2]:
Channel pulse length nominal  
[
3]:
Channel pulse length tolerance time  
[
4]:
Pause pulse length nominal  
[
5]:
Pause pulse length tolerance time  
[
6]:
Timeout tolerance time

### p17570[0...9] BI: SITOP PSE200U terminal S pulses signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8037 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Aux cct monit), VECTORGL (Aux cct monit), VECTOR3P (Aux cct monit), VECTORSL (Aux cct monit), VECTORM2C (Aux cct monit), VECTORDM (Aux cct monit), A_INFMV (Aux cct monit), B_INFMV (Aux cct monit), A_INFM2C (Aux cct monit), BMM2C (Aux cct monit) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source to receive pulses from terminal S for SITOP PSE200U.  
The pulses from terminal S can be received via a digital input.  
The status is displayed via r17571.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F49260, F49261, F49262, F49263, F49264

**Note:**
  
SITOP PSE200U selectivity module  
Using this module, the 24 V output voltage generated by the regulated power supply
can be distributed over 4 load circuits. The circuits are monitored and the status
signaled using terminal S.

|  |  |  |

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Monitoring enabled | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Channel 1 without fault | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Channel 2 without fault | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Channel 3 without fault | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Channel 4 without fault | No | Yes | - |

### p17745[0...n] Auxiliary fan switchover operating hours difference

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9838 |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P (Aux fan ctrl), VECTORM2C (Aux fan ctrl), VECTORDM (Aux fan ctrl), A_INFMV (Aux fan ctrl), B_INFMV (Aux fan ctrl), A_INFM2C (Aux fan ctrl), BMM2C (Aux fan ctrl) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [h] | 4294967295 [h] | [ 0 ] 500 [h] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the difference of the operating hours to switchover between the auxiliary fans.  
The auxiliary fans are automatically switched over if the operating hours of both
fans differ by more than the set value.

| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P (Aux fan ctrl), VECTORM2C (Aux fan ctrl), VECTORDM (Aux fan ctrl), A_INFMV (Aux fan ctrl), B_INFMV (Aux fan ctrl), A_INFM2C (Aux fan ctrl), BMM2C (Aux fan ctrl) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [h] | 100000 [h] | [ 0 ] 30000 [h] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20BASIC%20INFEED%20CONTROLMV.md#sinamics-alarms-basic-infeed-controlmv) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum operating time of the auxiliary fan.  
An appropriate alarm is output after the maximum operating time has been reached.  
Monitoring is deactivated with p17746 = 0.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49146
